dialogue_line_replacements.text: Prefiero Barcelona. Es más tranquila que Madrid y el clima es mejor.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: I prefer Barcelona. It's calmer than Madrid and the weather is better.

dialogue_line_replacements.text: Es verdad. Madrid es la ciudad más grande de España.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: That's true. Madrid is the largest city in Spain.

dialogue_line_replacements.text: En mi opinión, la tortilla de patatas de mi abuela es la mejor de España.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: In my opinion, my grandmother's Spanish omelette is the best in Spain.

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: In Spain, debating which city or region is better (Madrid vs. Barcelona, etc.) is a beloved national pastime. Comparatives are essential for these conversations.
cultural_notes_overrides.gestures: Tilting the head side to side while weighing two options is a common gesture in Spain.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: Spain uses 'guay' for cool/awesome in informal superlatives: 'Es lo más guay'. 'Molar' (to be cool) creates its own superlative: 'Es lo que más mola'.
